exec_args.query: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table';

args.query:
SELECT "Index", 
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days,
       COUNT(*) AS total_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Date" >= '2018-01-01' AND "Date" <= '2018-12-31'
  AND "Index" IN ('IXIC','NYA','GSPTSE')
GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql:
SELECT "Index", 
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days,
       SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" = "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS flat_days,
       COUNT(*) AS total_days
FROM index_trade
WHERE "Date" >= '2018-01-01' AND "Date" <= '2018-12-31'
  AND "Index" IN ('IXIC','NYA','GSPTSE')
GROUP BY "Index";

result.preview:
"The North American indices with more up days than down days in 2018 were:

- IXIC (NASDAQ): 44 up days vs 31 down days
- GSPTSE (S&P/TSX): 44 up days vs 41 down days"
